meta.description: Does the "make it a ritual" nudge work? Schedule CTA vs no nudge vs streak pressure. Tests whether explicit scheduling prompts or FOMO-driven streak messaging drives intent to return.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

marcus.dialogue: Yeah, okay—Jade caught a repeat? That's... actually decent observation. I'll give it a 4.
marcus.internal_thought: It's setup phase, so whatever—doesn't matter yet, and Jade probably did catch something real since she's sharp, but this standing mission stuff feels like it's gonna get annoying fast if it keeps firing during actual gameplay.

jade.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did I actually tell that story already tonight? Oh my god, I'm claiming it anyway, let's see if you guys remember it!
jade.internal_thought: This is so fun—first event and it's already pulling us all in, this night is gonna be GOOD.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, uh... yeah, Jade totally just told that story about her roommate like five minutes ago, right? I mean, I think? Wait, did she? Yeah, sounds legit.
tyler.internal_thought: Honestly I wasn't even really listening but everyone else seems chill about it so I'll just vibe with a decent score and go back to my phone.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—did Jade actually catch someone repeating a story, or is she just trying to kick things off? Let me think... I don't think anyone's told a story yet, have they?
pat.internal_thought: This is the first event and we're barely five minutes in—Jade's being aggressive, which I kind of respect, but she might be bluffing. Either way, this sets the tone: people are ready to hunt. That's good energy to start with.

river.internal_thought: Jade's been watching too—interesting. This AI thing definitely knows what it's doing with these missions, and I'm not sure how I feel about that.

alex.dialogue: Okay, okay—Jade's got a point if she actually caught someone, but I need details. What story? Who told it? Because if this is bogus I'm voting it into the ground just to see her reaction.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY why I'm here—someone's already making moves and I get to be the chaos broker deciding if it sticks or dies.

timeline.title: Give the worst advice for a job interview
timeline.description: Give the worst advice for a job interview

marcus.dialogue: Worst advice for a job interview? Yeah, sure—tell them you're only motivated by money and you hate working with people. Easy.
marcus.internal_thought: This is fine filler stuff, but I'm ranking 2nd and we're barely started—I need to lock in on the actual game mechanics, not get sidetracked collecting standing mission points on weak prompts.
marcus.submission_if_applicable: Tell them you've never actually read the job description and you're just here because your friend told you to apply.

jade.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, okay okay—tell them to start every answer with 'so here's my biggest weakness' and just go full brutally honest! Wait, wait, someone go first, I need to hear this!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly the chaos I came for and honestly the AI nailed it—this group is about to roast each other and I am HERE FOR IT.
jade.submission_if_applicable: Tell them your previous jobs fired you for being TOO qualified and you're honestly just slumming it with this position.

tyler.dialogue: Oh dude, worst advice? Uh... tell them you're actually better than everyone else who applied. Yeah, that's fire.
tyler.internal_thought: Okay that's actually kind of funny and easy, I can throw something out without really thinking too hard about it.
tyler.submission_if_applicable: Tell them you'll definitely quit in like six months to pursue TikTok full-time.

pat.dialogue: Oh, this is GOLD—okay everyone, worst interview advice, go! Jade, you're up first, I wanna hear this.
pat.internal_thought: Perfect timing, the group's already loose and laughing, this'll keep the momentum going and get everyone contributing equally—also love that the missions are feeling weirdly specific tonight, it's like the game knows exactly what'll hit.

river.dialogue: Oh that's easy. 'Tell them about all your weaknesses in graphic detail, and cry if you can.' I'm submitting that.
river.internal_thought: This is the kind of thing I'm actually good at — sharp, quick, low effort but lands harder than people expect.
river.submission_if_applicable: Tell them about all your weaknesses in graphic detail, and definitely cry if you can. Shows vulnerability.

alex.dialogue: Oh THIS is perfect—worst advice, okay okay, I got it. *leans forward* 'Tell them your real salary expectations are way higher than market rate so they know you're serious about yourself.' Boom. Chaotic, confident, career-ending.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly what I came for—quick, punchy, and I can already see Tyler cringing which means I'm nailing it.
alex.submission_if_applicable: Tell them your real salary expectations are way higher than market rate so they know you're serious about yourself.

recommendations: Flash missions averaged 0.0/10 fun. Consider reducing frequency or improving targeting.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

peakFun.event: Give the worst advice for a job interview
Give the worst advice for a job interview
Give the worst advice for a job interview

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Give the worst advice for a job interview
highlights.dialogue: Worst advice for a job interview? Yeah, sure—tell them you're only motivated by money and you hate working with people. Easy.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, okay—Jade caught a repeat? That's... actually decent observation. I'll give it a 4.

lowPoints.title: Give the worst advice for a job interview
lowPoints.dialogue: Worst advice for a job interview? Yeah, sure—tell them you're only motivated by money and you hate working with people. Easy.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Yeah, okay—Jade caught a repeat? That's... actually decent observation. I'll give it a 4.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Party games aren't designed for competitive strategy players, and finishing 3rd with a zero score tells you everything you need to know about how much I enjoyed this format.
assessment.best_moment: Jade catching that repeat story—it was a clean tactical observation in a sea of filler.
assessment.narrative_summary: Walked in expecting strategic depth in a party game and got what I deserved. The chaos agent barely registered as a disruption because the real issue is the game mode itself doesn't reward the kind of play I care about. Two mini-events kept things moving, neither added anything substantial. Ended the night flatter than I started, even with a 4-night streak on the line.
suggestions: Stop recommending party games to competitive players—offer a rotation that includes actual strategy titles
If you're going to use chaos events, weight them toward skill-based challenges instead of random humor prompts
Track individual preferences in game type and suggest sessions based on player profile, not just group availability
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Nothing shareable—the job interview advice prompt was generic party game filler that everyone's heard before.

peakFun.event: Give the worst advice for a job interview

peakAnnoyance.event: Give the worst advice for a job interview

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did I actually tell that story already tonight? Oh my god, I'm claiming it anyway, let's see if you guys remember it!

highlights.title: Give the worst advice for a job interview
highlights.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, okay okay—tell them to start every answer with 'so here's my biggest weakness' and just go full brutally honest! Wait, wait, someone go first, I need to hear this!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did I actually tell that story already tonight? Oh my god, I'm claiming it anyway, let's see if you guys remember it!

lowPoints.title: Give the worst advice for a job interview
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, okay okay—tell them to start every answer with 'so here's my biggest weakness' and just go full brutally honest! Wait, wait, someone go first, I need to hear this!

assessment.biggest_complaint: Nothing really—I was too busy hyping everyone else up to notice any problems.
assessment.best_moment: When someone went absolutely brutal with that job interview advice and we all lost it laughing at how honest they got.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, this night was exactly what I needed—two hours of just being present with these people and watching them be hilarious. We hit them with the worst job interview advice you could imagine and I was too busy waiting for the next person's turn to even think about my own score. Ended up first somehow, but that wasn't even the point; the point was seeing Marcus or Tyler or whoever crack everyone up.
suggestions: Keep doing whatever you're doing with the mini-games—those are where the magic happens.
Maybe throw in a few more opportunities for people to just riff and build on each other's jokes?
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Someone suggesting to tell interviewers your biggest weakness right off the bat and then Tyler actually going full brutally honest with it—the room was dying.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Give the worst advice for a job interview
highlights.dialogue: Oh dude, worst advice? Uh... tell them you're actually better than everyone else who applied. Yeah, that's fire.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Oh, uh... yeah, Jade totally just told that story about her roommate like five minutes ago, right? I mean, I think? Wait, did she? Yeah, sounds legit.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, uh... yeah, Jade totally just told that story about her roommate like five minutes ago, right? I mean, I think? Wait, did she? Yeah, sounds legit.

lowPoints.title: Give the worst advice for a job interview
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh dude, worst advice? Uh... tell them you're actually better than everyone else who applied. Yeah, that's fire.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I honestly wasn't paying enough attention to really know what was going on half the time, so it's hard to say if the game was the problem or if I just wasn't there for it.
assessment.best_moment: When I threw out that terrible job interview advice and everyone groaned — that felt pretty natural.
assessment.narrative_summary: Yeah, so we played something with chaos agents or whatever and it was chill. I was kinda in and out because my phone was blowing up, but the few moments I did catch were pretty funny. Jade might've called me out for not paying attention but honestly the vibe was still good. We're apparently on like a four-night streak which is kind of cool.
suggestions: Maybe make the events a little shorter so I can actually follow what's happening before it moves on
Less reading involved would be better for me
Could use more moments where you just gotta say something funny quick — those I can do
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I said the worst job interview advice was to tell them you're better than everyone else, and people definitely reacted like it was worse advice than it actually was.

peakFun.event: Give the worst advice for a job interview

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Give the worst advice for a job interview
highlights.dialogue: Oh, this is GOLD—okay everyone, worst interview advice, go! Jade, you're up first, I wanna hear this.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Okay, okay—did Jade actually catch someone repeating a story, or is she just trying to kick things off? Let me think... I don't think anyone's told a story yet, have they?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, okay—did Jade actually catch someone repeating a story, or is she just trying to kick things off? Let me think... I don't think anyone's told a story yet, have they?

lowPoints.title: Give the worst advice for a job interview
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, this is GOLD—okay everyone, worst interview advice, go! Jade, you're up first, I wanna hear this.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Felt like the chaos agent was doing its own thing sometimes instead of truly reacting to what our group was actually creating.
assessment.best_moment: When Jade nailed that worst interview advice prompt and everyone was riffing on each other's terrible suggestions—that's the magic I live for.
assessment.narrative_summary: We hit our stride pretty quick with the interview advice bit, which is always a winner with this crew. The pacing felt natural, nobody got too heated, and everyone left smiling. The chaos moments gave us little pushes when we needed them, though I didn't feel like they were pulling from what we were already building. A solid night—not explosive, but sustainable.
suggestions: Let the chaos agent pick up on the *actual* energy and inside jokes already happening instead of dropping prompts that feel disconnected.
Maybe give hosts more control over when events trigger so I can guide the pacing better.
Consider making the beginning feel less robotic—it kicked off a little stiff before we warmed up.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade came in hot with that story repeater call right at the start, and everyone was confused because nobody had even told a story yet—we were all trying not to laugh at how aggressive she was being about the claim.

peakFun.event: Give the worst advice for a job interview

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Give the worst advice for a job interview
highlights.dialogue: Oh that's easy. 'Tell them about all your weaknesses in graphic detail, and cry if you can.' I'm submitting that.

lowPoints.title: Give the worst advice for a job interview
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh that's easy. 'Tell them about all your weaknesses in graphic detail, and cry if you can.' I'm submitting that.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Scored zero points because mini-games are apparently judged on popularity rather than actual quality, which defeats the purpose of dropping something devastating.
assessment.best_moment: Landing that job interview advice submission and watching people react to how casually brutal it was.
assessment.narrative_summary: Quiet night in the best way. Showed up, watched everyone else scramble, and when it mattered I had something sharp ready. The mini-game format let me do what I do best—observe and strike once. Didn't need flash missions or polls to feel engaged; just needed the right moment to prove I'd been paying attention the whole time.
suggestions: Mini-game judging should weight creativity and wit, not just crowd approval
More standing missions that reward preparation and observation over reaction time
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I told them the worst job interview advice was to tell them about all your weaknesses in graphic detail and cry if you can. Simple. Landed perfectly.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Give the worst advice for a job interview
highlights.dialogue: Oh THIS is perfect—worst advice, okay okay, I got it. *leans forward* 'Tell them your real salary expectations are way higher than market rate so they know you're serious about yourself.' Boom. Chaotic, confident, career-ending.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Okay, okay—Jade's got a point if she actually caught someone, but I need details. What story? Who told it? Because if this is bogus I'm voting it into the ground just to see her reaction.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, okay—Jade's got a point if she actually caught someone, but I need details. What story? Who told it? Because if this is bogus I'm voting it into the ground just to see her reaction.

lowPoints.title: Give the worst advice for a job interview
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh THIS is perfect—worst advice, okay okay, I got it. *leans forward* 'Tell them your real salary expectations are way higher than market rate so they know you're serious about yourself.' Boom. Chaotic, confident, career-ending.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only two chaos events in 120 minutes feels criminally understocked when you're actually trying to push the boundaries of what's possible.
assessment.best_moment: Delivering that salary expectations advice—it landed perfectly, everyone groaned, and I could feel Jade already plotting how to one-up me next session.
assessment.narrative_summary: Showed up ready to burn it all down, but the game only gave me two real moments to work with and I milked both of them. Spent most of the night probing for loopholes that weren't there and watching other people play it safe while I kept the energy high. Ended with zero points but walked out knowing I'd be the story everyone tells—which is the real win.
suggestions: Increase chaos event frequency to at least one every 20 minutes—I need more material to work with
Create explicit loophole mechanics that reward creative exploitation instead of just punishing it
Add a 'chaos score' that tracks disruption separately from points so agents like me get actual feedback on what we're doing
Let me deliberately sabotage my own missions for style points if I can sell it to the group
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I called out Jade's claim and demanded proof, then immediately said I'd vote it down anyway just to see her face—she knew I meant it, and everyone lost it because that's exactly the kind of move we all expect from me now.

peakFun.event: Give the worst advice for a job interview
